skill.name: research-article-weekly
skill.description: Generates a weekly academic literature report based on keywords using PubMed. Use when the user wants to track recent research progress on a specific topic, automatically retrieving, classifying, and summarizing relevant papers from the last 7 days.
skill.summary: Automated weekly PubMed literature search with classification and AI-enhanced report generation.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 37 static findings. Over 30 are false positives (weak crypto flagged on argparse, argparse, and YAML frontmatter; certificate/key files flagged on standard function calls; system reconnaissance flagged on XML parsing and documentation). The remaining true positives (network access to PubMed API, external command execution via documented scripts) are legitimate and expected behavior for this research literature skill. No credential access, obfuscation, exfiltration, or injection found. The skill is safe for publication with low risk.

low_findings.title: Network Access to External API (PubMed)
low_findings.description: The Python script makes HTTP requests to the public PubMed NCBI API (eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov). These calls fetch article metadata and abstracts. No credentials or sensitive data are transmitted. This is expected behavior for a PubMed literature search skill.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution via Documented Workflow
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md documents shell commands (python scripts/pubmed_search.py) that the AI agent executes to perform PubMed searches. Arguments are user-supplied keywords passed through argparse, which prevents command injection. This is legitimate and expected for a script-driven skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' on lines containing argparse.ArgumentParser, YAML frontmatter, and JSON field names. These are not cryptographic operations. The scanner likely matched on substring patterns unrelated to cryptography.

low_findings.title: Certificate/Key File Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Certificate/key files' on function calls with dot notation (args.keywords). These are standard argparse namespace attribute accesses, not certificate or key file references.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'System reconnaissance' on XML parsing operations and documentation lines describing workflow parameters. These are standard data processing operations and documentation, not reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Heuristic Dangerous Combination (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Code execution + Network + Credential access' combination. While code execution (external commands) and network access exist, there is NO credential access. The PubMed API is public and requires no authentication. No environment variables, API keys, tokens, or passwords are read or transmitted.

content.user_title: Generate Weekly Research Literature Reports from PubMed
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually searching PubMed and organizing papers each week. This skill automates the full workflow, retrieving recent articles by keyword, classifying them into research categories, and producing a structured summary report.
actual_capabilities: Searches PubMed for articles published in the last 7 days using user-specified keywords
Classifies retrieved articles into research categories: Fundamental, Applied, Methodology, Review, and Other
Generates a structured Markdown report with categorized articles and summaries
Supports AI-enhanced refinement for better summarization and topic extraction
Outputs results in both JSON and Markdown formats
limitations: Limited to PubMed-indexed literature only, not other databases like Web of Science or Scopus
Rule-based classification may mislabel articles that span multiple research categories
Requires Python 3.10+ and the requests library to be installed in the execution environment
Maximum of 20 articles per search to keep reports focused and readable

use_cases.title: Weekly Research Monitoring
use_cases.description: A PhD student sets a keyword for their research area and runs the skill weekly to stay updated on the latest publications without manually searching PubMed each time.

use_cases.title: Literature Review Preparation
use_cases.description: A principal investigator preparing a grant application uses the skill to gather recent publications in their field, classified by research type for easy reference.

use_cases.title: Cross-Disciplinary Awareness
use_cases.description: A research scientist monitoring adjacent fields uses multiple keyword searches to track emerging methods and tools that could apply to their own work.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PubMed Literature Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search PubMed for recent articles about CRISPR gene therapy published in the last 7 days.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick check for new publications on a specific topic

prompt_templates.title: Structured Report with Keywords
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a weekly research report for keyword 'machine learning in healthcare'. Include classification into research types and provide a summary for each category.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive weekly monitoring of a research field

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Keyword Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Run literature searches for both 'quantum computing' and 'post-quantum cryptography' separately. Compare the volume and types of research in each area over the last week.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comparing research activity across related topics

prompt_templates.title: Custom Report with AI Enhancement
prompt_templates.prompt: Search PubMed for 'climate change adaptation' from the last 7 days. After generating the draft report, refine it by grouping articles by topic tag and highlighting the most significant findings for each research category.
prompt_templates.scenario: Polished deliverable-ready research brief for team or stakeholder review

output_examples.input: Generate a weekly research report for keyword 'Alzheimer's disease'
output:
# Weekly Research Report: Alzheimer's disease

## Executive Summary
This week's research on Alzheimer's disease shows a strong focus on Applied Research, particularly new diagnostic methods and therapeutic approaches. 18 relevant articles were identified across 4 research categories.

## Applied Research
1. **Early Detection of Alzheimer's Using Plasma Biomarkers** (Journal of Alzheimer's Disease)
   A study evaluating plasma p-tau217 as a screening tool for early-stage Alzheimer's in a cohort of 500 patients.

2. **Phase II Trial of Novel Aducanumab Analog** (New England Journal of Medicine)
   Clinical trial results for a next-generation anti-amyloid antibody with improved BBB penetration.

## Fundamental Research
3. **Tau Propagation Mechanisms in Microglial Cells** (Nature Neuroscience)
   In vitro study demonstrating microglia-mediated tau spread in organotypic brain slice cultures.

output_examples.input: Search PubMed for recent papers on mRNA vaccine technology
output:
## Methodology & Tools
1. **Novel Lipid Nanoparticle Formulation for mRNA Delivery** (ACS Nano)
   Development of a ionizable lipid library screened for enhanced mRNA delivery to hepatic targets.

2. **Self-Amplifying mRNA Platform for Rapid Vaccine Development** (Molecular Therapy)
   Description of a replicon-based mRNA platform reducing required dose by 10x compared to conventional mRNA.

## Review & Survey
3. **mRNA Vaccines: A Comprehensive Review of Platforms and Clinical Applications** (Nature Reviews Drug Discovery)
   Systematic review covering 50+ clinical trials of mRNA-based vaccines beyond COVID-19.

best_practices: Use specific, well-defined keywords for better search relevance. Combine related terms with Boolean operators when the skill supports it.
Review the rule-based classification and manually reclassify any articles that seem miscategorized before sharing the report.
Run the skill on the same day each week (e.g., Monday morning) for consistent weekly coverage.
anti_patterns: Do not use overly broad keywords like 'cancer' or 'biology' which will return hundreds of unfiltered results.
Do not treat the rule-based classification as final for publication without human review of each category assignment.
Do not use this skill for systematic reviews or meta-analyses that require exhaustive database searches across multiple sources.

faq.question: What databases does this skill search?
faq.answer: This skill searches PubMed only. It does not search Web of Science, Scopus, Google Scholar, or other databases.

faq.question: How far back does the search go?
faq.answer: By default, the skill searches for articles published in the last 7 days. You can adjust this with the --days parameter.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: No. PubMed's E-utilities API is free and publicly accessible without authentication.

faq.question: Can I get results in a format other than Markdown?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports both JSON and Markdown output formats using the --format parameter.

faq.question: How does the article classification work?
faq.answer: Articles are classified using keyword matching on titles and abstracts into five categories: Fundamental Research, Applied Research, Methodology and Tools, Review and Survey, and Other.

faq.question: What are the system requirements?
faq.answer: Python 3.10 or later with the requests library installed. The script is tested on Linux and macOS.
